description:
Offered: every semester
This seminar course supports teacher candidates while completing the Candidate Assessment of Performance (CAP) required for teacher licensure in Massachusetts. Candidates share their practicum experiences with peers and faculty. This course provides candidates with knowledge and skills to communicate and collaborate with educational personnel and students’ family/guardians. Various models of co-teaching are examined. In addition, candidates create resumes and are prepared for interviews.
Prerequisite(s): Successful Passage of Stage Two Education Unit Review.
Note: This course is required for all Education Initial Licensure Majors
Corequisite(s): The practicum associated with the candidate’s major
prerequisites: Successful Passage of Stage Two Education Unit Review.
